project.name: No-Terminal Assisted Draft
project.purpose: Draft a lightweight onboarding plan for a small team evaluating ni before installing the CLI.

non_goals.title: Do not execute downstream work from this draft.
Do not call a model API or web service from this example.
Do not treat model-authored skills or model judgment as authoritative.

capabilities.title: Draft a human-readable onboarding plan without claiming readiness.

requirements.title: Keep the docs/plan draft and .ni/contract.json draft aligned.
Leave readiness, locking, hash verification, and prompt compilation to full ni.

decisions.title: Use no-terminal assisted mode only for learning and drafting.
Choose the trusted runner that will validate the draft.

risks.title: A reader may mistake model confidence for deterministic readiness.
risks.mitigation: Keep the no-terminal checklist, blocker questions, and CLI graduation requirement visible.

evaluations.title: No-terminal boundary review.
evaluations.method: Review docs and draft contract for explicit assisted status, visible blocker questions, and absence of lock or prompt compilation claims.

open_questions.title: Who will run ni status, ni end, and ni run before any implementation handoff?
Which project will be used as the first real validation target?
